artifact_name: Bounded Paid Pilot Outreach Email v0.2
status: internal outreach draft, not legal-reviewed, not buyer-ready final copy
purpose: Introduce one focused paid diagnostic pilot without upper-tier pricing, strategic-license language, or external-validation claims.
standing_boundary_line: SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics is a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review process. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, or guarantee of AI safety.
subject_options: Focused diagnostic review for one AI-agent workflow
Mapping AI-agent workflow failure points before scale
Pre-deployment diagnostic for agentic workflow risk
email_body:
Hi [Name],

AI-agent workflows can appear ready while still acting on weak evidence, stale system state, unclear authority, or unverified completion claims.

SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics offers a focused paid diagnostic pilot for one concrete AI-agent workflow. The review uses selected synthetic scenarios to map where evidence handling, scope boundaries, handoffs, escalation thresholds, and recovery paths may need design attention before broader rollout.

This is a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review process. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, or guarantee of AI safety.

Would a short scoping call be useful to identify one workflow where this first-pass review could create practical value?

Best,
Benjamin J. Clark
Founder, SpecShift Labs
shorter_email_body:
Hi [Name],

SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics offers a focused paid diagnostic pilot for one concrete AI-agent workflow, mapping where evidence handling, scope boundaries, handoffs, escalation thresholds, or recovery paths may need design attention before broader rollout.

This is a pre-deployment diagnostic, not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, or safety guarantee.

Would a short scoping call be useful to identify one workflow worth reviewing?

Best,
Benjamin J. Clark
Founder, SpecShift Labs
do_not_include_in_first_outreach: validated without the local schema qualifier
benchmark, certification, compliance, security, deployment-ready, proof, or guarantee language
follow_up_offer: If the recipient responds, offer a 20-minute scoping call focused on one workflow, its inputs, system boundaries, approval points, handoffs, and failure consequences.
claim_boundary: This outreach email stops fitting if it is edited to imply validated performance, certification, compliance sign-off, runtime protection, deployment readiness, strategic licensing, or guaranteed safety.
